event: The Virgin of Mercy (Pokrov) sheltering the faithful under her mantle
symbols: outspread mantle — Mary's intercessory protection over the faithful (the Pokrov tradition)
red garment — royalty and the Passion of Christ, borne also by his mother
gathered faithful beneath the mantle — the Church sheltered by Mary's prayers
golden halo — sanctity and divine favor
diverse figures (clergy, laity, children) — the universality of Mary's maternal care for all the baptized
description: A Ukrainian or Slavic Orthodox icon depicting the Virgin of Mercy (Pokrov). The Virgin Mary stands centrally, wearing a red maphorion over a blue garment, her arms outstretched. Her large mantle, held open by her hands, extends to cover a crowd of faithful believers gathered beneath it. The figures include men, women, and children in various postures of prayer and devotion. Some wear clerical vestments, others are laypeople. A golden halo encircles Mary's head. The background is dark, emphasizing the luminous figures.
theological_description: The Virgin of Mercy (Pokrov) icon embodies the theology of Mary as intercessor and protector of the Church. The image draws on Revelation 12:1, the woman clothed with the sun, who in patristic and liturgical tradition is identified both with the Church and with the Virgin Mary. Her outstretched mantle sheltering the faithful visualizes the prayer of intercession, reflecting the theological conviction expressed at the wedding at Cana (John 2:1-5) that Mary brings the needs of the people before her Son. The gathered faithful beneath her protection recall Psalm 91:4, where God covers with his feathers and under his wings one finds refuge, here mediated through the maternal figure of the Theotokos. The icon also evokes Ruth 3:9, where the spreading of the garment signifies protection and covenant faithfulness. In Eastern theology, the Pokrov celebrates the vision of Saint Andrew the Fool, who saw Mary spread her veil over the congregation in the Blachernae church in Constantinople, a manifestation of her perpetual intercession before the throne of God (Hebrews 7:25, applied analogically). The diverse community beneath the mantle reflects Galatians 3:28, the unity of all believers under one protective grace.

scripture_refs.reason: The woman clothed with the sun, identified in tradition with Mary as mother and protector of the Church
Mary's intercessory role, bringing the needs of the faithful before Christ
Divine protection imaged as shelter under wings, here mediated through Mary's mantle
The spreading of the garment as a sign of protection and covenant faithfulness
The unity of diverse believers gathered under one protective grace
